execution_boundary.summary: SLSA defines supply-chain assurance requirements rather than executing transitions.

preparation_boundary.summary: SLSA provenance can inform artifact preparation review without authorizing deployment or use.

commitment_boundary.summary: Provenance and build-level evidence can support artifact standing at commitment time.

semantic_equivalence_boundary.summary: Builder, source, provenance, and level semantics require normalized mapping.

unknown_trajectory_boundary.summary: SLSA does not classify unknown runtime action paths.

authority_boundary.summary: Supply-chain assurance does not establish runtime action authority.

evidence_freshness_boundary.summary: Specification version, provenance, builder identity, and verification state require freshness checks.

reconstruction_boundary.summary: SLSA provenance can strengthen reconstruction of source, build, and artifact history.

interoperability_path.summary: SLSA evidence enters as artifact provenance and build-assurance evidence.

next_required_action: add valid-provenance and unverifiable-builder fixture cases
